idea-plugin.name: Rails View
idea-plugin.description:
Provides a Rails View like the one removed from RubyMine 2025.3 — and goes further. A dedicated Rails View tab in the Project tool window replaces the raw filesystem tree with a Rails-aware view of your project.

Sections

Models · Controllers · Views · Helpers · Mailers · Jobs · Services · Channels · GraphQL · Policies · Serializers · Decorators · Uploaders · Assets · JavaScript · Config · Database · Lib · Spec / Test · Project Files · External Libraries · Scratches and Consoles

Models

  • Files shown by Ruby class name (User, OrderItem, …)
  • Database columns from db/schema.rb listed directly under each model
  • Associations (has_many, belongs_to, …), scopes, and attribute macros
  • typed_store blocks expanded to show each individual typed attribute
  • Methods grouped into Class Methods, Instance Methods, and Private Methods

Controllers

  • Inline Views › … shortcut linking directly to the matching views directory
  • Matching helper file shown as an expandable child node
  • Partials collected under a Partials folder
  • Actions listed with their matching view files as children

Database

  • Migrations sorted newest-first
  • Filenames reformatted for readability: 2026-06-17-113427 CreateNats

Routes

  • Parses config/routes.rb and displays all routes with HTTP verb and path
  • Nested path view: api › v1 › users folder hierarchy (or switch to a flat controller list)
  • Supports resources / resource, namespace, concern, match, and all HTTP verb helpers

External Libraries & Scratches

  • External Libraries: shows installed gems and the Ruby SDK — identical to RubyMine’s built-in node
  • Scratches and Consoles: shows IDE scratch files and console history — identical to RubyMine’s built-in node
  • Both are opt-in via Settings → Tools → Rails View and fully positionable in the section order

Navigation

  • Auto-scroll from editor keeps the tree in sync as you move between files
  • Select in Rails View action available in the editor context menu

Configurable

Fine-tune the view under Settings → Tools → Rails View:

Display Section Options

  • Show or hide the Routes section
  • Show or hide Spec / Test sections
  • Show or hide the Project Files node
  • Show or hide the External Libraries node (gems + SDK)
  • Show or hide the Scratches and Consoles node

Section Behaviour Options

  • Toggle the Views shortcut under each controller
  • Toggle method grouping (Class / Instance / Private)
  • Toggle model macro grouping (Schema / Associations / Scopes / Attributes)
  • Toggle nested vs. flat route display

Use the up/down arrows in Settings to reorder sections, or commit a .railsview file to the project root to share the layout with your team. Every node participates in the order. Prefix a key with ! (e.g. !spec) to hide that section for everyone, overriding individual Settings.

action.description: Locate the current file in the Rails View pane
